---
title: "Perry_data_clean_05_12_2026"
format: html
---


```{r setup}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(purrr)
library(tibble)
library(readr)
library(aqp)

# Source the SAME shared module used by NCSS_clean_pedon_pack.qmd.
# This guarantees that the index calculators, horizon-name parser,
# and pedon-pack assembler are byte-for-byte identical between datasets.
source("../code/pedon_pack_functions_05_02_2026.R")
``` 

```{r load-raw}
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Load Perry field-pedon data
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
perry_fp <- read_csv("../data/Perry_FP_data_cleaned_updated_series_05_11_26.csv",
                     show_col_types = FALSE)

glimpse(perry_fp)
```

```{r normalize-taxonomy}
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Normalize taxonomy strings (mirror NCSS conventions) and parse subgroup
# modifiers into taxsubgrp_mod1 / mod2 / mod3.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Reuse the same parse_subgrp_mods used in the NCSS script.
parse_subgrp_mods <- function(subgrp, gg_hint = NA_character_) {
  x0 <- subgrp %>% as.character() %>% str_squish() %>% na_if("")
  if (is.na(x0)) {
    return(tibble(
      subgrp_gg = gg_hint %||% NA_character_,
      taxsubgrp_mod1 = NA_character_,
      taxsubgrp_mod2 = NA_character_,
      taxsubgrp_mod3 = NA_character_
    ))
  }
  toks <- str_split(x0, "\\s+", simplify = FALSE)[[1]]
  toks <- toks[toks != ""]
  if (length(toks) == 0) {
    return(tibble(
      subgrp_gg = gg_hint %||% NA_character_,
      taxsubgrp_mod1 = NA_character_,
      taxsubgrp_mod2 = NA_character_,
      taxsubgrp_mod3 = NA_character_
    ))
  }
  gg   <- if (!is.na(gg_hint) && str_squish(gg_hint) != "") gg_hint else toks[length(toks)]
  mods <- if (length(toks) >= 2) toks[1:(length(toks) - 1)] else character(0)
  tibble(
    subgrp_gg      = gg,
    taxsubgrp_mod1 = mods[1] %||% NA_character_,
    taxsubgrp_mod2 = mods[2] %||% NA_character_,
    taxsubgrp_mod3 = mods[3] %||% NA_character_
  )
}

perry_fp_clean <- perry_fp %>%
  mutate(
    Point          = as.character(Point),
    Horizon        = as.character(Horizon),
    To.Depth..cm.  = as.character(To.Depth..cm.),

    taxclname   = na_if(str_squish(as.character(taxclname)),   ""),
    taxorder    = na_if(str_squish(as.character(taxorder)),    ""),
    taxsuborder = na_if(str_squish(as.character(taxsuborder)), ""),
    taxgrtgroup = na_if(str_squish(as.character(taxgrtgroup)), ""),
    taxsubgrp   = na_if(str_squish(as.character(taxsubgrp)),   ""),

    # Match NCSS: taxpartsize is lowercase
    taxpartsize = str_to_lower(na_if(str_squish(as.character(taxpartsize)), ""))
  ) %>%
  mutate(parsed = map2(taxsubgrp, taxgrtgroup, parse_subgrp_mods)) %>%
  unnest_wider(parsed) %>%
  mutate(taxgrtgroup = coalesce(taxgrtgroup, subgrp_gg)) %>%
  select(-subgrp_gg)
```

```{r parse-depths}
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Parse horizon depth bounds.
#
# Perry's `To.Depth..cm.` column is a "top-bottom" string ("0-15", "15-30", ...).
# When only top is parseable, fall back to top + H.Depth.cm. for bottom.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

parse_top_bottom <- function(x) {
  top <- as.numeric(str_extract(x, "^\\d+"))
  bot <- as.numeric(str_extract(x, "(?<=-)\\d+"))
  tibble(top_cm = top, bottom_cm = bot)
}

perry_with_depths <- perry_fp_clean %>%
  bind_cols(parse_top_bottom(.$To.Depth..cm.)) %>%
  mutate(
    hzdept = as.numeric(top_cm),
    hzdepb = if_else(
      !is.na(bottom_cm),
      as.numeric(bottom_cm),
      as.numeric(top_cm) + as.numeric(H.Depth.cm.)
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(hzdept), is.finite(hzdepb), hzdepb > hzdept)
```

```{r parse-horizons}
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Parse horizon designations using the SHARED parser.
# Produces hzname_clean, L_master, L_suffix, hz_base in the SAME label
# space as NCSS — this is what makes prop_hzbase_* features comparable.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

perry_hz_parsed <- perry_with_depths %>%
  bind_cols(
    parse_hznames(.$Horizon) %>%
      select(hzname_clean, L_master, L_suffix, hz_base)
  ) %>%
  rename(hzname = Horizon)
```

```{r parse-color}
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Convert Munsell matrix color -> R, G, B in [0, 1].
#
# IMPORTANT: must use the same scale as NCSS (where col2rgb output / 255 was
# stored). The NCSS path was: munsell_hex -> col2rgb / 255. We replicate that
# exactly here, instead of using parseMunsell(return_triplets = TRUE) whose
# output convention may differ slightly.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

fix_munsell <- function(x) {
  x %>%
    as.character() %>%
    str_squish() %>%
    # split fused forms like "10YR4/3" -> "10YR 4/3"
    str_replace("^([0-9.]+[A-Z]+)([0-9.]+/[0-9.]+)$", "\\1 \\2")
}

perry_hz_color <- perry_hz_parsed %>%
  mutate(
    munsell      = fix_munsell(Matrix.Color),
    munsell      = na_if(munsell, ""),
    munsell_hex  = aqp::parseMunsell(munsell)
  )

idx <- which(!is.na(perry_hz_color$munsell_hex) & perry_hz_color$munsell_hex != "")
rgb_mat <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow(perry_hz_color), 3,
                  dimnames = list(NULL, c("R", "G", "B")))
rgb_mat[idx, ] <- t(grDevices::col2rgb(perry_hz_color$munsell_hex[idx])) / 255

perry_hz_color <- perry_hz_color %>%
  mutate(R = rgb_mat[, "R"], G = rgb_mat[, "G"], B = rgb_mat[, "B"])
```

```{r assemble-hz-table}
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Assemble Perry horizon-level table conforming to PEDON_PACK_HZ_SCHEMA.
#
# Decisions on missing or differently-named columns:
#
#   peiid           <- Perry "Point" (string)
#   sandtotmeasured <- lab_sand            (assumed % by mass, 0-100)
#   silttotmeasured <- lab_silt            (assumed % by mass, 0-100)
#   claytotmeasured <- lab_clay            (assumed % by mass, 0-100)
#   texcl           <- lab_texture_class
#   cec7            <- NA_real_            (not measured for full Perry set;
#                                           see notes below)
#
# CEC NOTE
# --------
# We have CEC for only a subset of Perry pedons, and on that subset the
# values are below the kandic 16-cmol(+)/kg-clay threshold. Two options were
# considered:
#
#   (1) Stamp Perry Bt horizons with a low cec7 to encode the kandic
#       assumption. REJECTED — this would silently inject the kandic prior
#       into great-group prediction (essentially hardcoding the answer for
#       Kandiudult vs. non-Kandi great groups).
#
#   (2) Leave cec7 as NA. The training recipe's step_impute_median will fill
#       with the NCSS training median at predict time. The model will treat
#       CEC-derived features (cec7_w, min_cec_per_kg_clay_0_150, etc.) as
#       low-information signals for these pedons. This is the most honest
#       option and the one we use. Document the kandic assumption in the
#       interpretation of results, not in the input data.
#
# We additionally surface `pct_hz_with_cec7` (a per-pedon coverage proxy)
# from build_pedon_pack(), so the model and downstream analysis can see how
# CEC-poor each pedon is.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

perry_hz_table <- perry_hz_color %>%
  transmute(
    peiid           = as.character(Point),
    hzdept          = as.numeric(hzdept),
    hzdepb          = as.numeric(hzdepb),
    hzname          = as.character(hzname),
    hzname_clean    = as.character(hzname_clean),
    hz_base         = as.character(hz_base),
    L_master        = as.character(L_master),
    L_suffix        = as.character(L_suffix),
    texcl           = as.character(lab_texture_class),
    sandtotmeasured = as.numeric(lab_sand),
    silttotmeasured = as.numeric(lab_silt),
    claytotmeasured = as.numeric(lab_clay),
    cec7            = NA_real_,
    R               = as.numeric(R),
    G               = as.numeric(G),
    B               = as.numeric(B)
  )
```

```{r assemble-ped-table}
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Assemble Perry pedon-level table conforming to PEDON_PACK_PED_SCHEMA.
# One row per peiid. Carries taxonomy labels (used as ground truth for
# external validation, NOT as predictors).
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

perry_ped_table <- perry_fp_clean %>%
  group_by(Point) %>%
  summarise(
    taxonname_clean = first_non_na(series_clean) %>% str_to_title(),
    taxorder        = first_non_na(taxorder),
    taxsuborder     = first_non_na(taxsuborder),
    taxgrtgroup     = first_non_na(taxgrtgroup),
    taxsubgrp_mod1  = first_non_na(taxsubgrp_mod1),
    taxsubgrp_mod2  = first_non_na(taxsubgrp_mod2),
    taxsubgrp_mod3  = first_non_na(taxsubgrp_mod3),
    taxpartsize     = first_non_na(taxpartsize),

    # Optional location columns - default NA when Perry has no coordinates
    POINT_X = if ("POINT_X" %in% names(perry_fp_clean))
                first_non_na(POINT_X) %>% as.numeric() else NA_real_,
    POINT_Y = if ("POINT_Y" %in% names(perry_fp_clean))
                first_non_na(POINT_Y) %>% as.numeric() else NA_real_,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  rename(peiid = Point) %>%
  mutate(peiid = as.character(peiid)) %>%
  distinct(peiid, .keep_all = TRUE)
```

```{r validate-and-restrict}
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Validate against the shared schema, then restrict to pedons present in both
# the horizon table and the pedon table (mirrors NCSS handling).
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

perry_hz_table  <- perry_hz_table  %>% filter(peiid %in% perry_ped_table$peiid)
perry_ped_table <- perry_ped_table %>% filter(peiid %in% perry_hz_table$peiid)

validate_hz_schema(perry_hz_table,   source_name = "Perry horizon table")
validate_ped_schema(perry_ped_table, source_name = "Perry pedon table")

cat("Perry pedons:    ", nrow(perry_ped_table), "\n")
cat("Perry horizons:  ", nrow(perry_hz_table),  "\n")
cat("Mean hz/pedon:   ",
    round(nrow(perry_hz_table) / nrow(perry_ped_table), 1), "\n")
```

```{r build-pedon-pack}
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Build perry_pack_150 using the IDENTICAL shared builder used by NCSS.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

perry_pack_150 <- build_pedon_pack(
  hz_table     = perry_hz_table,
  ped_table    = perry_ped_table,
  max_depth_cm = 150,
  source_name  = "Perry"
)

glimpse(perry_pack_150)
```

```{r schema-compare}
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Compare Perry pack columns against the NCSS pack.
# This is the diagnostic that catches future drift between the two pipelines.
#
# - Columns present in NCSS but missing in Perry  -> features the model
#   trained on but Perry can't supply (will need pad_like_train_raw).
# - Columns present in Perry but missing in NCSS  -> harmless but noteworthy.
# - prop_hzbase_* columns will commonly differ in coverage; the values
#   themselves are now in the same label space because both use parse_hznames.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

ncss_pack_path <- list.files("../outputs", pattern = "^pedon_pack_150_.*\\.csv$",
                             full.names = TRUE)

if (length(ncss_pack_path) > 0) {
  ncss_pack <- read_csv(ncss_pack_path[which.max(file.mtime(ncss_pack_path))],
                        show_col_types = FALSE)

  in_ncss_only  <- setdiff(names(ncss_pack), names(perry_pack_150))
  in_perry_only <- setdiff(names(perry_pack_150), names(ncss_pack))
  in_both       <- intersect(names(ncss_pack), names(perry_pack_150))

  cat("Columns in NCSS only (", length(in_ncss_only),  "):\n  ",
      paste(in_ncss_only,  collapse = ", "), "\n", sep = "")
  cat("Columns in Perry only (", length(in_perry_only), "):\n  ",
      paste(in_perry_only, collapse = ", "), "\n", sep = "")
  cat("Columns in both (",  length(in_both),       "):\n  ",
      paste(in_both,       collapse = ", "), "\n", sep = "")

  # Numeric columns: compare distributions
  num_cols <- intersect(in_both,
                        names(ncss_pack)[sapply(ncss_pack, is.numeric)])

  feat_compare <- map_dfr(num_cols, function(nm) {
    n_vals <- ncss_pack[[nm]]
    p_vals <- perry_pack_150[[nm]]
    tibble(
      feature           = nm,
      ncss_pct_nonNA    = round(mean(!is.na(n_vals)) * 100, 1),
      perry_pct_nonNA   = round(mean(!is.na(p_vals)) * 100, 1),
      ncss_median       = round(median(n_vals,  na.rm = TRUE), 3),
      perry_median      = round(median(p_vals,  na.rm = TRUE), 3),
      ncss_iqr          = round(IQR(n_vals,     na.rm = TRUE), 3),
      perry_iqr         = round(IQR(p_vals,     na.rm = TRUE), 3)
    )
  }) %>%
    mutate(coverage_gap = ncss_pct_nonNA - perry_pct_nonNA) %>%
    arrange(desc(abs(coverage_gap)))

  cat("\nTop 20 features by NCSS-vs-Perry coverage gap:\n")
  print(feat_compare %>% slice_head(n = 20), n = 20)
} else {
  message("No NCSS pedon_pack_150 file found in ../outputs/. ",
          "Run NCSS_clean_pedon_pack.qmd first to enable schema comparison.")
}
```

```{r save-outputs}
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Save Perry outputs.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

write.csv(perry_hz_table,
          paste0("../outputs/perry_hz_table_", Sys.Date(), ".csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)

write.csv(perry_ped_table,
          paste0("../outputs/perry_ped_table_", Sys.Date(), ".csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)

write.csv(perry_pack_150,
          paste0("../outputs/perry_pack_150_", Sys.Date(), ".csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)

# Also save the cleaned long-form Perry data (used downstream for
# horizon-level validation / building per-pedon truth).
write.csv(perry_fp_clean,
          paste0("../outputs/perry_fp_clean_", Sys.Date(), ".csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)
```
